document_info.title: The FA Concussion Guidelines
document_info.subtitle: If In Doubt, Sit Them Out: Concussion Guidelines for the Education Sector and Football
document_info.target_audience: Coaches, Teachers, Parents, Team Managers, and Match Officials
document_info.core_principle: If In Doubt, Sit Them Out

executive_summary.core_message: Concussion must be taken seriously to safeguard the long-term health and welfare of players. These guidelines outline how to recognize a potential concussion, immediate actions to take for removal from play, and a structured, safe protocol for returning to academic and sports activities.

recognizing_concussion.definition: A concussion is a traumatic brain injury caused by a direct or indirect blow to the head, face, neck, or elsewhere on the body, causing an impulsive force transmitted to the head.
visible_clues_signs: Lying motionless on the ground or slow to get up
Unsteady on feet, balance problems, or falling over
Confused, unaware of play, or answering questions slowly
Injury or trauma visible on face or head
symptoms_reported_by_player: Headache or pressure in the head
Feeling sluggish, hazy, foggy, or groggy
Feeling unusually emotional, irritable, or sad
red_flags_immediate_emergency_action: Weakness or tingling/burning in arms or legs
Loss of consciousness or deteriorating conscious state
Increasingly restless, agitated, or combative behavior

immediate_management_protocol.step_1: Remove the player from play immediately if a concussion is suspected.
immediate_management_protocol.step_2: Do not leave the player alone. Ensure they are monitored by a responsible adult constantly.
immediate_management_protocol.step_3: Do not allow the player to drive a motor vehicle or ride a bicycle.
immediate_management_protocol.step_4: If any 'Red Flags' are present, arrange immediate transfer to an emergency department / hospital.

principles: A player must be completely symptom-free before entering the physical stages of the GRTP protocol.
Each stage must last for a minimum of 24 hours.
If any symptoms return during a stage, the player must drop back to the previous symptom-free stage and rest for 24 hours before attempting to progress again.

stages.activity_allowed: Complete physical and cognitive rest. Minimize screen time (phones, TV, computers) and reading. School attendance may need modification.
Light walking, jogging, or stationary cycling at low intensity. No resistance training, no head impact, no twisting movements.
Running drills, ball-tracking drills, and basic passing arrays. No heading, no tackling, no contact scenarios.
More complex training patterns, passing combinations, and coordinated tactical drills. Resistance training can be re-introduced.
Requires formal medical clearance. Participation in normal training sessions, competitive match simulations, tackling, and heading activities.
Full clearance for competitive match selection and standard physical exertion.

return_to_education_framework.core_thesis: Cognitive recovery must take priority over sports recovery. A student athlete must successfully return to a full academic workload without symptoms before progressing to the advanced physical stages of the GRTP.
academic_modifications: Shorter school days or frequent rest breaks during lessons
Avoidance of loud or highly stimulating environments (music class, noisy cafeterias)
Extensions on assignments, homework deadlines, and postponement of formal examinations

the_triad_communication_model.image_description: A structural flowchart showing three interconnecting nodes arranged in a cycle: 'School/Teacher', 'Parent/Guardian', and 'Football Club/Coach'. Arrows point bidirectionally between all three groups to illustrate that information regarding a player's symptoms, academic behavior, and physical status must be shared fluidly to safely manage recovery.

tactical_layout.central_focus: The Safety of the Child / Player

reporting_lines.Club-to-Parent: Coach reports the initial injury incident, symptoms observed, and removal from play.
reporting_lines.Parent-to-School: Parents notify teachers of the concussion to activate temporary academic accommodations.
reporting_lines.School-to-Parent-to-Club: School tracks cognitive tolerance, reporting symptom clearance to parents before physical training resumes at the club.
